title: Skymet Weather Update June 12: Monsoon Set To Cover More Parts Of India; Rain, Dust Storm And Heatwave Alerts Issued - www.skymetweather.com
full_text:
Skymet Weather Update June 12: Monsoon Set To Cover More Parts Of India; Rain, Dust Storm And Heatwave Alerts Issued
Key Takeaways:
- The Southwest Monsoon is likely to advance into more parts of Central, Eastern and Southern India over the next 2-3 days.
- Heavy rainfall is expected over Kerala, Coastal Karnataka, Sikkim, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal and parts of Northeast India.
- Thunderstorms and dust storms may affect Delhi, Haryana, North Rajasthan and West Uttar Pradesh.
- Heatwave conditions are likely to continue over parts of Rajasthan and Vidarbha.
The Weather Systems Over The Country
Conditions are favourable for the further advance of the Southwest Monsoon into some more parts of the Central Arabian Sea, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, the remaining parts of Tamil Nadu and the Southwest Bay of Bengal, some more parts of the West-Central and Northwest Bay of Bengal, and West Bengal, as well as parts of Chhattisgarh, Bihar, Jharkhand and Odisha during the next 2 to 3 days.
The Western Disturbance is seen as a trough in middle tropospheric westerly winds, with its axis at 5.8 km above mean sea level, running roughly along Longitude 70°E to the north of Latitude 30°N.
A cyclonic circulation is over Northwest Uttar Pradesh and adjoining areas, extending up to 1.5 km above mean sea level.
The seasonal trough at mean sea level is extending from Punjab to Gangetic West Bengal across Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand.
Another cyclonic circulation is over East Uttar Pradesh.
A trough is extending from the cyclonic circulation over East Uttar Pradesh to Vidarbha across Chhattisgarh and East Madhya Pradesh at lower levels.
Weather Activities During The Last 24 Hours
During the last 24 hours, moderate to heavy rain occurred over the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
Light to moderate rain with one or two heavy spells occurred over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim and Northwest Uttar Pradesh.
Light to moderate rain occurred over West Bengal, Assam, Meghalaya, Manipur, Tripura, parts of Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, North Punjab, Uttarakhand and Goa.
Light rain occurred over Odisha, Northeast Madhya Pradesh and parts of Konkan and Goa.
Heatwave conditions prevailed over parts of Rajasthan, Southwest Uttar Pradesh, Vidarbha and parts of Madhya Pradesh.
Forecast
During the next 24 hours, moderate to heavy rain may occur over Kerala, Coastal Karnataka, the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim and West Assam.
Light to moderate rain may occur over Northeast India, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, the Western Himalayas, East Madhya Pradesh, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu.
Thunderstorms and dust storms may occur over parts of North Rajasthan, Delhi, Haryana and West Uttar Pradesh.
Light scattered rain may occur over Konkan and Goa, Vidarbha and Marathwada.
Isolated light rain is possible over South Gujarat.
Heatwave conditions may persist over parts of Rajasthan and Vidarbha.
